metadata.description: Six-block evaluation of FIGS interpretability tradeoffs: (A) arity comparison, (B) arity significance, (C) accuracy equivalence, (D) Pareto frontier, (E) path length, (F) cognitive complexity.

unsigned_spectral_vs_random_oblique.note: performance = balanced_accuracy for classification, r2 for regression

unsigned_spectral_vs_hard_threshold.note: performance = balanced_accuracy for classification, r2 for regression

unsigned_spectral_vs_axis_aligned.note: performance = balanced_accuracy for classification, r2 for regression

F_cognitive_complexity.interpretation: Cognitive complexity = path_length x arity measures total features inspected per prediction. For a 4-split path: arity 1.8 => ~7.2 feature values inspected; arity 3.0 => ~12.0 feature values. Lower CC means the model is easier for domain experts to understand.
